name: aligned_zero_size
reason: aligned with size 0 should be meaningless/false; if true, the spec fails to guard against division by zero

name: odd_aligned_to_even
reason: 1 is odd and cannot be 2-aligned; if provable the alignment definition is unsound

name: aligned_implies_double_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_implies_double_size(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
    ensures
        aligned(a, 2 * b),
{
}
reason: alignment to b does not imply alignment to 2*b (e.g. 4 is 4-aligned but not 8-aligned); would signal the spec is too weak

name: adjacent_preserves_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_4_adjacent_preserves_alignment(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        b > 1,
        aligned(a, b),
    ensures
        aligned(a + 1, b),
{
}
reason: if a is b-aligned then a+1 must not be (for b>1); provability would mean alignment is trivially true

name: all_addresses_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_all_addresses_aligned(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(a, b),
{
}
reason: not every address is aligned to every nonzero size; if provable the spec is vacuously true and useless
